Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Determine SQL Approach Need to count events per case_id Filter cases where count less than or equal to 6 Join or use a subquery or CTE to get the full event rows for those cases Options WITH clause CTE to calculate counts then join back to original table HAVING clause in a subquery then join Window function COUNT OVER PARTITION BY case_id and filter IN subquery

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: CTE is clean and readable WITH case_counts AS SELECT case_id COUNT as event_count FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id HAVING COUNT less than or equal to 6 SELECT e star FROM event_log e JOIN case_counts cc ON e case_id equals cc case_id
